text: Wandering through these polished, modern spaces, I am struck by the interplay between transparency and enclosure. The glass walls divide yet display, almost inviting yet holding me apart, and I feel an odd blend of anticipation and caution. Inside, the curated lights and colors of the R+D exhibit draw me in. Here, surrounded by the deliberate architecture of knowledge-sharing, I am reminded of how observation changes both the observer and the observed. It’s strangely exhilarating: a place engineered for thought and innovation, yet it leaves me reflecting on the barriers—seen and unseen—that channel our curiosity. I feel present, yet distanced, both participant and spectator in this intellectual theater.
